mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the consumer price index signals deflationary pressure that raises real debt burdens and erodes corporate pricing power, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with negative month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration that compresses consumer spending and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 45 percent of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real retail sales and industrial production signal a coordinated demand-supply contraction that forces inventory liquidation and widespread earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO are not followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of joint-occurrence months over a 3-year rolling window, or a regression interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the unemployment rate exceeds 6 percent, a month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary easing that reduces corporate borrowing costs and stabilizes equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6 percent, month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1 percent signals acute manufacturing contraction that triggers negative earnings surprises and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals improving construction activity and credit market thawing that lifts forward earnings estimates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 50 percent of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment signals expanding household income and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are followed by positive 20-day returns in less than 55 percent of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP signals recessionary income contraction that reduces corporate revenues and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances over any 5-year rolling window.
